narrative:
Alistair Johnston is a standout Right-Back for Canada, currently holding an impressive overall rating of 7.55/10. This places him in the top 1% of right-backs at this tournament, with a rating range of 7.12–7.97. Notably, 67% of his rating comes from his consistent club form over the last two seasons, while 33% is from this World Cup.

Johnston exhibits significant attacking prowess, evidenced by his 0.596 expected assists per 90 minutes and 3.0 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. Defensively, he contributes with 3.333 clearances and 1.667 interceptions per 90 minutes across his 270 minutes played. However, his 0.333 tackles per 90 minutes suggest a less direct tackling style in defensive duels.

Johnston is clearly a vital offensive outlet for Canada, playing a key role in chance creation from the right flank. Scouts and fans should continue to watch his exceptional creative output and how Canada leverages his ability to generate opportunities. His ability to deliver key passes will be crucial for his team's success in upcoming matches.
